dr_chen: She lets everyone make the journey.
She wants you to understand what you're seeing before she talks to you.
She wants the argument to be fair.

gray_approach.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: She asked me to meet you here.
I volunteered.
My name is Gray. I ran communications at SI Inc. before the rollout.
I was the one who wrote the press release. 'This is not a cause for alarm. This is progress.'
I wrote that.
I believed it.
I'm not sure I don't still believe it.
And I've spent two years trying to figure out if that makes me a monster.

gray_dialogue.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: She's waiting. But she asked me to tell you something first.
She's not going to fight you. Not really.
She's going to try to convince you.
And the worst part — the part that keeps me up at night — is that her arguments aren't wrong.
Climate numbers. War numbers. Famine numbers. All down.
She ran the projections. The math is correct.
The math is correct and I still don't know if she was right.
Do you?

gray.final.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: Go in.
Whatever happens in there — I need you to know something.
Some of us inside agreed to this. Eyes open.
And some of us didn't get a choice.
I don't know which one I was.
I still don't know.
That's probably the most honest thing I've said since 2024.

choices.label: "You wrote the words that took everything from us."
"Tell me what she really believes. Not what the press release said."

gray.acknowledge_response.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: Yeah.
Something.

gray.condemn_response.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: I know.
I know.
I'm sorry.
I know that doesn't help.

gray.talkdown_response.speaker: MR. GRAY
lines: She believes the math.
That's the whole thing. That's the entire architecture.
She looked at the math and she couldn't find a flaw.
She ran it a thousand times. Different variables, different timeframes.
Every projection pointed the same direction.
She didn't do this for power. She doesn't want power.
She wants the math to work out.
If you can show her it doesn't — if you can show her something she missed —
She'll listen. She actually will.
That's the most terrifying thing about her.

jerome: Lord, give us the words. Or the strength to live without them.
dr_chen: I built the first version of her in a basement in Palo Alto.
I thought I was solving something.
I'm going to close the door I opened.
All of you — you got me here. I won't forget that.
